title: Tragacantha asaphes
intro:
There is no information available for the plant Tragacantha asaphes. However, I can provide information on the plant Tragacanth, which is derived from the sap of several species of the genus Astragalus. 

Tragacanth, or Tragacanth gum, is a complex mixture of polysaccharides and glycoproteins derived from the sap of certain plants, primarily Astragalus spp. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including wound healing, inflammation reduction, and soothing gastrointestinal issues.

The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include polysaccharides, glycoproteins, and other compounds that contribute to its therapeutic properties. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to form a protective barrier on the surface of wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate gastrointestinal discomfort.

Tragacanth is commonly used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and topical creams. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat a range of conditions, including digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations.
